identity.variant: Standard (one of Standard / Advanced / MaxA / MaxB / Showroom / Showroom Compute Edition configurations documented in the official manual)
identity.reason: KUAVO 4Pro is still listed as a current product on Leju's official EN (lejurobot.com/en) and ZH (lejurobot.com/zh) sites in 2026, with an active official manual describing the Standard configuration in detail (dimensions, DoF, battery, compute). KUAVO 5 / 5-W is a newer, parallel product line — Leju's own site presents it alongside 4Pro under the same 'General Humanoid Robot Series' without any explicit successor/end-of-life statement for 4Pro, so it is not marked superseded.

evidence.evidenceSummary: Manual describes a 6-DoF-per-leg bipedal walking platform.

22 DoF total (5 DoF/arm, 6 DoF/leg), excluding end-effector.

60V/6Ah swappable battery, ≥500 charge cycles.

kuavo_sdk is a ROS1-based interface package with Python3 examples; unified control interface for locomotion, audio, and image processing.

identity.variant: TALOS (base platform; head and gripper are customizable)
identity.reason: TALOS appears on PAL Robotics' current official product index (pal-robotics.com/robots/) in 2026 with active 'Request a quote' / 'Download Datasheet' CTAs, its own product page and datasheet. PAL's own KANGAROO blog describes KANGAROO as building upon 'previous humanoid platforms like REEM-C and TALOS,' but PAL has not published any explicit discontinuation or successor statement for TALOS itself, so it remains current rather than superseded.

identity.variant: Base platform with selectable 4/5/7-DoF arm configurations (each a distinct, officially documented row in the datasheet)
identity.reason: KANGAROO appears on PAL's current official index (pal-robotics.com/robots/) with active product and datasheet pages. Its 4/5/7-DoF arm configurations are clearly distinguishable by payload package on the official datasheet, so per-configuration loadRatings should be kept (not treated as an unresolved conflict) — this corrects DATA-R01's over-cautious treatment. A 'Kangaroo PRO' dual-arm variant was reported in third-party press (2025 Automatica launch) but was not found as a distinct row on the currently-open official product/datasheet pages, so it is not registered separately.

evidence.evidenceSummary: Product page literally states 'Height: 1,58 m' (158cm); the official datasheet PDF text extraction instead reads 1.60 m. Both are official PAL documents; treated as an unresolved minor conflict (rounding or datasheet-revision difference) pending clarification with PAL.

evidence.sourceTitle: Kangaroo | Biped Humanoid Robot for Locomotion Research
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official material gives only a 50-65kg range depending on arm/end-effector configuration; no single per-configuration weight figure was found, so a scalar value cannot be confidently assigned.
Datasheet lists max speed 2 m/s, consistent with product page's dynamic-locomotion framing.

Torso (2) + legs (6×2) are fixed at 14; arms (4/5/7 DoF ×2) and hand end-effector choice (parallel gripper 1 DoF, Seed RH8D 7 DoF, Inspire RH56 6 DoF) push family total up to ~40. No single scalar applies to 'KANGAROO' as a whole.
Datasheet and product-page text both state 3h autonomy.

identity.variant: EDU (one of Lite / EDU / Super / Pro(175cm-Standard) columns on the official spec-comparison table)
identity.reason: Oli remains LimX Dynamics' current general-purpose humanoid line ('continuously iterated and optimized' per the official product page), with EDU as one of four distinguishable configurations on the current official spec table. IMPORTANT CORRECTION vs. the existing repo record: the current official spec table places EDU at 31 active DoF and ~2h battery life — the repo's registered dof=33 and runtimeMin=90 actually match the 'Super' column, not 'EDU'. This looks like a column mix-up in the prior data pass and is flagged as a conflict below.

evidence.evidenceSummary: Bipedal humanoid (legs 6 DoF each) common to all Oli variants.
Spec table: EDU column height 165cm, same as Lite and Super; the larger 175cm variant is a separate column (Pro/Standard).
Spec table gives an upper-bound '≤55kg' figure, not an exact weight.

evidence.variant: Oli EDU (shared across all Oli variants)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Max speed 5km/h is identical across all four spec-table columns.

Confirmed twice independently: current spec table lists Active DoF = 31 for both Lite and EDU columns; 33 is the Super column's value and 43 is the Pro/Standard column's value. The repo's existing record of dof=33 for 'Oli EDU' matches the Super column, not EDU — flagged as a correction needed.

Current spec table: battery life ~2h for Lite & EDU, ~1.5h for Super & Pro. The repo's existing runtimeMin=90 for 'Oli EDU' matches the Super/Pro ~1.5h figure, not EDU's ~2h — flagged as a correction needed.
9,500mAh capacity and charger spec are identical across all four columns.

evidence.value: Self-developed bilingual (CN/EN) voice interaction; remote control; upper/lower-level motion APIs
evidence.evidenceSummary: Product page confirms voice interaction system and motion API layers.

evidence.value: Modular SDK; full Python development support; open upper/lower-level APIs; URDF; Isaac Sim/MuJoCo/Gazebo compatible
evidence.evidenceSummary: Confirmed on product page.

evidence.value: Motion Control: RK3588/8G/64G (confirmed common to Lite/EDU/Super); Perception: '–' for EDU on spec table (only Super/Pro standard-equip Orin NX 157TOPS/16G)
evidence.evidenceSummary: The current official spec table shows a dash ('–') in EDU's Perception-compute cell (i.e., no standard Orin NX unit), while Super and Pro have Orin NX (157TOPS) standard. The repo's existing record attributes an Orin NX 157TOPS/16GB/1TB unit to Oli EDU's computePlatform, which conflicts with this reading. One earlier extraction pass hedged that Orin NX might be an optional (non-standard) add-on for EDU specifically; this ambiguity is unresolved and needs manual review directly against the live table.

loadRatings.condition: Official 'Maximum Load (Single Arm)' figure; identical across Lite/EDU/Super columns.

currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: Sensor suite for perception-based motion control.

currentFeatures.feature: Bilingual (CN/EN) voice interaction system

conflicts.description: Repo's dof=33 for Oli EDU matches the current spec table's 'Super' column, not 'EDU' (31). Recommend correcting to 31 or re-verifying which variant should be registered as 'Oli EDU'.
Repo's runtimeMin=90 for Oli EDU matches the current spec table's 'Super'/'Pro' column (~1.5h), not EDU's ~2h (120min).
Repo attributes an Orin NX 157TOPS perception unit to Oli EDU; current spec table shows a dash (not standard) for EDU's Perception column, with Orin NX standard only on Super and Pro columns.

unresolved: Whether the registered robotId 'limx-oli' (name 'Oli EDU') should in fact correspond to the spec table's 'Super' column given the dof/runtime match, or whether EDU's true current values are 31 DoF / ~2h runtime / no standard Orin NX — needs a data-correction decision, not further research.
humanReviewRequired: Resolve the EDU vs Super column mismatch on dof, runtimeMin, and computePlatform before next publish.
